references.text: Coode, MJE & R Weibel. 1994. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: notes, new taxa and combinations in sect. Elaeocarpus: 1. Kew Bulletin 49: 235-259.
Coode, MJE. 1983. A conspectus of Sloanea (Elaeocarpaceae) of the Old World. Kew Bulletin 38: 347-427.
Coode, MJE. 2001. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: the E. stipularis complex, E. nitidus group & E. barbulatus. Kew Bulletin 56: 513-565.
Coode, MJE. 2001. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: the verticellatae subgroup of the Monocera group and a new Philippine species. Kew Bulletin 56: 885-901.
Coode, MJE. 2007. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: E. forbesii, a Philippine species with unusual features, and some possible 'associates'. Kew Bulletin 62: 321-328.
Coode, MJE. 2010. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: new taxa and understanding in the Ganitrus group. Kew Bulletin 65: 355-399.
Coode, MJE. 2019a. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: the Oreocarpus group in Malesia. Kew Bulletin 74: 35.
Coode, MJE. 2019b. Elaeocarpus for Flora Malesiana: five new taxa. Kew Bulletin 74: 36.
Coode, MJE. 2024. New infraspecific taxa and combinations in Malesian Elaeocarpus. Kew Bulletin 79: 47-54.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 120 ; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11; Type: from Java. = Elaeocarpus wenzelii Merr. , PJS 9c (1914) 371 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 20; Type: Wenzel 365 (PNH, holo, lost?; G, L, US, iso), Leyte: Leyte, Dagami, in forests, c. 60m, 13-Jul-1913. = Elaeocarpus sphaericus (Gaertn.) K.Schum. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl) 3(6) (1890) 5, nom. illeg. ; Corner, Gard. Bull. Sing. 10 (1939) 326; ≡ Ganitrus sphaerica Gaertn. , Fruct. Sem. Pl. 2 (1791) 271.

species.citation: Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 26 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 14; Type: Elmer 6237 (US, iso), Luzon: Benguet, Mt Sto Tomas, May-1904. = Elaeocarpus parvilimbus Merr. , J. Arnold Arbor. 32 (1951) 168; Type: BS 42239 Ramos (A, holo; A, SING, iso), Luzon: Rizal, Mt Lumutan, Apr-1923. = Elaeocarpus affinis Merr. , PJS 9c (1914) 372 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 14; Type: Wenzel 788 (US, iso), Leyte: Leyte, Masaganap near Jaro, in forests, c. 600m, May/Jul-1914. var. argenteus. Distribution: LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte, MINDORO. Notes: Exposed ridges on higher mountains. 1100-2250m. Native. var. cuernosensis (Elmer) Weibel, ined.; ≡ Elaeocarpus cuernosensis Elmer, LPB 4 (1911) 1173; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 15; Type: Elmer 9653 (E, K, L, NA, US, iso), Negros: Negros Oriental, Cuernos Mtns, Mar-1908. = Elaeocarpus laxirameus Elmer, LPB 4 (1911) 1186; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 17; Type: Elmer 11222 (BISH, BM, E, K, MO, NA, NY, US, syn), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, Todaya, Jul-1909; Elmer 11945 (BM, E, K, L, MO, NY, US, syn), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, Todaya, Oct-1909. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CAMIGUIN, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis, NEGROS. Notes: Ridges in the mossy forests. 1100-1500m. Native. var. elmeri (Aug.DC.) Weibel, ined.; ≡ Elaeocarpus elmeri Aug.DC., LPB 2 (1909) 637; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 16; Type: Elmer 8870 (L, NY, US, syn); Elmer 8967 (L, US, syn), Luzon: Benguet, Baguio, Mar-1907. = Elaeocarpus zambalensis Elmer, LPB 9 (1934) 3185, nom. inval., no Latin descr.; ex Merr., J. Arnold Arb. 32 (1951) 172; Type: Elmer 21943 (PNH, holo; L, MO, NY, iso), Luzon: Pampanga, Camp Stotsenburg, Mt Pinatubo, May-1927. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga. Notes: Thickets and forests. 1100-1400m. Native.

species.citation: (Blanco) Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 43 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 15; Blanco, Fl. Filip. (1837) 439; ≡ Vallea calomala Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 439; Type: Not extant, ‘Illustrative specimen’: Merr. Sp. Blancoanae 276, leg. M Ramos (US), Luzon: Rizal, Antipolo, 105m, 23-Feb-1914. = Elaeocarpus isotrichus (Turcz.) Fern.-Vill. , Fl. Filip., 3rd Ed. 4(Nov. App.) (1880) 31 ; Aug.DC. in Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 638; ≡ Monocera isotricha Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 19 (1846) 494; Type: Cuming 836, Luzon. = Elaeocarpus philippinensis Warb. , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 101 ; Aug.DC. in Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 636; Type: Cuming s.n., Luzon. = Elaeocarpus bataanensis Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 41; Type: FB 21908 Medina (US, iso), Luzon: Bataan, forests back of Limay, on forested slopes, c. 300m, 2-Jul-1914. ≠ Elaeocarpus oblongus auct. non Gaertn. ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 31; S.Vidal, Sinopsis Atlas (1883) 17; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 72; Perkins, Frag. Fl. Philip. (1904) 100; Merr., PJS 1 (1906) Suppl. 90; Aug.DC. in Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 636. var. calomala. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Abra (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Laguna, Rizal. Notes: Coode & Weibel (1994): “no correlated fruit and flower is known. Since there is no way to distinguish E. calomala var calomala from E. cumingii vegetatively (for which correlated fruit and flower is known), it is not clear if the fruit of var. calomala is known or not. Those fruiting sheets determined over the years as E. calomala all come from islands other than Luzon, or from provinces in Luzon other than these three”. 800m (photos). Native. var. pustulatus (Merr.) Weibel, Kew Bull. 49 (1994) 239; ≡ Elaeocarpus pustulatus Merr., PJS 7c (1912) 295; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 19; Type: BS 14635 Ramos (PNH, holo, lost; G, K, US, iso), Camiguin: Mt Mahinog, in forests, 11-Apr-1912. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CAMIGUIN, LUZON: Aurora, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, MINDORO, SIBUYAN. Notes: Elmer 12568 from Mt Giting-giting is tentatively assigned to this species by Coode & Weibel. Forest on slopes. Fl. Mar-May. 1200m. Native. var. villosiusculus (Warb.) Weibel, Kew Bull. 49 (1994) 240; ≡ Elaeocarpus villosiusculus Warb., Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 101; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 20; Type: Warburg 13071 (B, holo, lost; G, iso), Luzon: Quezon, Sampaloc. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Batangas, Camarines, Quezon. Notes: Collections other than the type has uncertainties in identification. Lowland forests. 200-400m. Native.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 121. = Elaeocarpus fissistipula Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 1(2) (1859) 210 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 16. var. brevipes (Merr.) Coode, Kew Bull. 56 (2001) 551; ≡ Elaeocarpus brevipes Merr., J. Straits Branch Roy. Asiat. Soc. 86 (1922) 327. = Elaeocarpus mindanaensis Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 150; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 18; Type: Clemens 910 (G, iso). Distribution: Borneo, Philippines. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Lanao. Notes: Forests. 1-900m. Native. var. longipetiolatus (Merr.) Coode, Kew Bull. 56 (2001) 555; ≡ Elaeocarpus longipetiolatus Merr., J. Straits Branch Roy. Asiat. Soc. 76 (1917) 96. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines. PALAWAN, TAWI-TAWI (photos). Notes: 0-50(-750)m. Native. var. nutans (R.Knuth) Coode, Kew Bull. 56 (2001) 553; ≡ Elaeocarpus nutans R.Knuth, Repert. Spec. Nov. Regni Veg. 44 (1938) 131; Type: from Borneo. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines. TAWI-TAWI. Notes: 20-100(-760)m. Native.
species.distribution: MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, TAWI-TAWI.

species.citation: Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind., Eerste Bijv. (1861) 407. ssp. procerus (Aug.DC.) Weibel, Kew Bull. 49 (1994) 253; ≡ Elaeocarpus procerus Aug.DC., LPB 2 (1909) 636; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 19; Type: Elmer 10158 (G, holo; BM, K, L, iso). Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS. Notes: Primary forest. 1200m. Native. ssp. fusicarpus (Elmer) Weibel, Kew Bull. 49 (1994) 253; ≡ Elaeocarpus fusicarpus Elmer, LPB 4 (1911) 1174; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 16; Type: Elmer 10819 (PNH, syn, lost; A, BM, G, K); 10828 (PNH, syn, lost; A, BM, G, K) & 11769 (PNH, syn, lost; A, BM, G, K). Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS. Notes: Forested slopes, damp situations. Fl. Mar-Apr, Jun; fr. Sep. (150-)500-1250m. Native. ssp. oliganthus (Merr.) Coode, Kew Bull. 49 (1994) 254; ≡ Elaeocarpus oliganthus Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 42; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 19; Type: FB 13398 Foxworthy, de Mesa & Villamil (PNH, holo, lost; G, K, iso), Mindanao: Zamboanga del Sur, Siay River (near Kabasalan), along the river near sea level. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur. Notes: L.L.Co: Known only from the type collection. Fl. & fr May. 0m. Native.
species.distribution: Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 28 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 19; Type: FB 26000 Ponce (PNH, holo, lost; K, US, iso), Mindanao: Surigao del Norte, near Mabini, in level rich soil at the edge of the forest, c. 50m, 11-May-1916.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Surigao, Surigao del Norte.
species.notes: Known only from the type (Mark Coode, pers. comm. 5-Aug-16). Lowland forests. 50m.

species.citation: Merr. & Quisumb. , J. Arnold Arbor. 32 (1951) 169; Type: PNH 10099 Sulit (A, holo; G, K, L 2 sheets, iso), Mindanao: Bukidnon, Mt Kitanglad, mossy forest, 1850m.
